artifact: apps/video-storefront — bounded adaptation: CTA 'Back this film' vs 'Buy film'

hard_gates.detail: Adaptation defined in bounded_adaptation_candidate in video-storefront.spec.json. Measure: conversion_rate delta, seed=42 cohort.
Variant cohort: identical KPIs to baseline (events=368, 13.0%, $5.72, 3.8%, guardrail_breach=false). CTA reverted to baseline.

dimensions.notes: Adaptation explicitly specified in app spec. Measure defined. Change traces directly to bounded_adaptation_candidate.
Only 2 default string values changed. Reverted after experiment. No scope expansion.
Variant cohort identical to baseline. guardrail_breach=false. Code restored to spec-locked state.
Key learning captured in iteration proof: synthetic cohort is copy-agnostic; CTA experiments need real traffic. Not yet written to lifecycle skill improvement log.

experiment_summary.hypothesis: Transactional framing ('Buy film') may improve conversion over patronage framing ('Back this film')
experiment_summary.nonclaim: Synthetic cohort only. Delta of 0.0% does not prove CTA copy has no effect — simulator is copy-agnostic by design.
experiment_summary.recommendation: Real-traffic A/B test required when deployed. Keep baseline CTA 'Back this film' locked until real signal available.

non_claims: does not prove real payment processing
does not prove CTA copy has measurable effect — synthetic cohort is copy-agnostic
synthetic cohort only — not real traffic or patron behavior
ITERATE verdict does not block lifecycle advance; experiment is complete at synthetic scope
